actions.name: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
actions.description: Block websites until a specific todo is marked complete. Use this only when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo, like 'block x.com until I finish my workout'.

parameters.name: websites
parameters.description: List of website hostnames to block.

parameters.name: todoId
parameters.description: ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.

parameters.name: todoName
parameters.description: Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.

parameters.name: unlockDurationMinutes
parameters.description: Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: Optional profile label for the block rule.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Block websites until a named todo is completed.

exampleCalls.user: Use BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BROWSER_ACTION
actions.description:
browser_action:
  purpose: Control a Chromium-based browser through the local runtime: launch, navigate, interact, inspect, execute JavaScript, wait, and manage tabs.
  provider_state: Read-only browser availability and recent action state are available from the computerState provider. Use state/info/list_tabs only for explicit live refreshes.
  flow: Open or connect first, then navigate and interact. Use clickables to discover interactive elements.
  actions: open/connect/close/navigate/click/type/scroll/screenshot/dom/get_dom/clickables/get_clickables/execute/state/info/context/get_context/wait/list_tabs/open_tab/close_tab/switch_tab.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Browser action to perform.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL for open, navigate, or open_tab.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: CSS selector for click, type, or wait.

parameters.name: coordinate
parameters.description: Viewport [x, y] coordinate for click.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text to type, text to click, or text to wait for.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: JavaScript source to execute in the page.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Scroll direction.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Scroll amount in pixels.

parameters.name: tabId
parameters.description: Tab identifier for tab actions.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout in milliseconds for wait actions.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Chromium browser control router: open/connect/navigate/click/type/read dom/clickables/execute/wait/tabs; read-only state.

exampleCalls.user: Use BROWSER_ACTION with the provided parameters.

actions.name: CLOUD_AGENT
actions.description: ElizaCloud agent ops router: provision a container, freeze (snapshot+stop) a running agent, resume a frozen agent from snapshot, or check credit balance and runtime estimate.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Which cloud-agent operation to run: 'provision', 'freeze', 'resume', or 'check_credits'.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name. Required for op='provision' and op='resume'.

parameters.name: project_name
parameters.description: Project identifier (lowercase, no spaces). Required for op='provision' and op='resume'.

parameters.name: containerId
parameters.description: Container ID. Required for op='freeze'.

parameters.name: snapshotId
parameters.description: Specific snapshot ID for op='resume' (defaults to latest).

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Optional description for op='provision'.

parameters.name: environment_vars
parameters.description: Additional environment variables for op='provision' or op='resume'.

parameters.name: auto_backup
parameters.description: Enable periodic auto-backup for op='provision' (default: true).

parameters.name: detailed
parameters.description: Include transaction history for op='check_credits'.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true to execute mutating ops ('provision', 'freeze', 'resume') after the preview.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Cloud agent ops: provision, freeze, resume, check credits.

exampleCalls.user: Use CLOUD_AGENT with the provided parameters.

actions.name: MANAGE_WINDOW
actions.description:
manage_window_action:
  purpose: Manage desktop windows: list visible windows, focus or switch, arrange or move, minimize, maximize, restore, and close.
  guidance: Use list first to discover window IDs, then use focused window actions.
  actions: list/focus/switch/arrange/move/minimize/maximize/restore/close.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Window action to perform.

parameters.name: windowId
parameters.description: Window identifier.

parameters.name: windowTitle
parameters.description: Window title or app-name query for switch/restore/focus operations.

parameters.name: arrangement
parameters.description: Layout for arrange: tile, cascade, vertical, or horizontal.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Target X coordinate for move.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Target Y coordinate for move.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Window management router: list/focus/switch/arrange/move/minimize/maximize/restore/close; list first to discover window ids.

exampleCalls.user: Use MANAGE_WINDOW with the provided parameters.

actions.name: PLAY_MUSIC_QUERY
actions.description: Handle any complex music query that requires understanding and research, then queue the selected track after confirmed:true. Supports: artist queries (first single, latest song, similar artists, popular songs, nth album), temporal (80s, 90s, specific years), genre/mood/vibe, activities (workout, study, party), charts/trending, albums, movie/game/TV soundtracks, lyrics/topics, versions (covers, remixes, acoustic, live), and more. Uses Wikipedia, music databases, and web search to find the right music.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true to resolve the music query and add the result to the queue.

actions.name: SPAWN_AGENT
actions.description: Spawn a specific task agent inside an existing workspace when you need direct control.

parameters.name: agentType
parameters.description: Specific task-agent framework to spawn. Options: claude (Claude Code), codex (OpenAI Codex), gemini (Google Gemini), aider, pi, shell (generic shell).

parameters.name: workdir
parameters.description: Working directory for the agent. Defaults to current directory.
parameters.descriptionCompressed: Spawn task agent in existing workspace for direct control.

parameters.name: task
parameters.description: Open-ended task or prompt to send to the task agent once spawned.

parameters.name: memoryContent
parameters.description: Instructions or shared context to write to the task agent's memory file before spawning.

parameters.name: approvalPreset
parameters.description: OPTIONAL permission preset. Leave UNSET for normal coding/research tasks — the runtime defaults to 'autonomous' which gives the agent full tools including shell, the helpers it needs to work effectively, and standard --dangerously-skip-permissions (the orchestrator runs in a sandbox so this is safe). Only set this when the user EXPLICITLY asks for a constrained agent: 'readonly' for a true audit-only review (no shell, no writes, no web), 'standard' or 'permissive' for unusual approval flows. Picking 'readonly' for normal tasks breaks bash helper scripts and is almost never what the user wants.

parameters.name: keepAliveAfterComplete
parameters.description: Keep the spawned task-agent session alive after a completed turn so it can receive another tracked task.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Spawn task agent in existing workspace for async coding/research; returns session id for follow-up.

exampleCalls.user: Use SPAWN_AGENT with the provided parameters.

actions.name: UNINSTALL_SKILL
actions.description: Uninstall a non-bundled skill. Bundled skills cannot be removed.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Installed skill slug or name to uninstall.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Remove non-bundled skill.

exampleCalls.user: Use UNINSTALL_SKILL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: USE_COMPUTER
actions.description:
use_computer_action:
  purpose: Control the local desktop for real application interaction when direct computer operation is required.
  guidance: Take a screenshot before acting. After each desktop action, the result includes a screenshot when available.
  actions: screenshot/click/click_with_modifiers/double_click/right_click/mouse_move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Desktop action to perform.

parameters.name: coordinate
parameters.description: Target [x, y] pixel coordinate.

parameters.name: startCoordinate
parameters.description: Start [x, y] pixel coordinate for drag.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text to type.

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description: Modifier keys to hold during click_with_modifiers, e.g. ['cmd', 'shift'] or ['ctrl'].

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Single key or combo string depending on action.

parameters.name: button
parameters.description: Mouse button for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: clicks
parameters.description: Number of clicks for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: scrollDirection
parameters.description: Scroll direction.

parameters.name: scrollAmount
parameters.description: Scroll tick count.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Desktop control router: screenshot/click/modified click/double/right/move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr; screenshot before acting.

exampleCalls.user: Use USE_COMPUTER with the provided parameters.

actions.name: USE_SKILL
actions.description: Invoke an enabled skill by slug. The skill's instructions or script run and the result returns to the conversation.
actions.descriptionCompressed: Invoke an enabled skill by slug.

actions.name: WALK_TO
actions.description: Walk to a coordinate or named destination. Provide either destination: name OR x: N, z: N.

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: Optional named destination (overrides x/z).

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Target world X coordinate.

parameters.name: z
parameters.description: Target world Z coordinate.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Optional reason logged with the walk.

exampleCalls.user: Use WALK_TO with the provided parameters.

actions.name: WALLET_PREPARE
actions.description: Prepare a non-binding wallet proposal. Set kind="swap" to fetch a BSC

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Proposal kind: "swap" for a BSC swap quote, "transfer" for a token transfer preview.

parameters.name: fromSymbol
parameters.description: Source asset symbol (swap only, e.g. "BNB", "USDT"). One of fromSymbol/toSymbol must be BNB on BSC.

parameters.name: toSymbol
parameters.description: Destination asset symbol (swap only, e.g. "BNB", "USDT"). One of fromSymbol/toSymbol must be BNB on BSC.

parameters.name: fromAddress
parameters.description: Source token contract address (swap only — required when the source asset is not BNB).

parameters.name: slippageBps
parameters.description: Slippage tolerance in basis points (swap only, default 300 = 3%).

parameters.name: toAddress
parameters.description: Recipient EVM address (transfer) or destination token contract address (swap, required when destination is not BNB). 0x-prefixed, 40 hex characters.

parameters.name: assetSymbol
parameters.description: Token symbol to transfer (transfer only, e.g. "BNB", "USDT", "USDC").

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Human-readable amount. For swaps the source-asset units (e.g. "0.5"); for transfers the asset units (e.g. "1.5", "100").

actions.descriptionCompressed: Wallet preview ops: swap, transfer.

exampleCalls.user: Use WALLET_PREPARE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: WORKFLOW_LIFECYCLE_OP
actions.description: n8n workflow lifecycle operation. Pass `op` ("activate", "deactivate", or "delete") and optionally `workflowId`. Identifies workflows by ID, name, or semantic description.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Lifecycle operation to perform. One of: activate, deactivate, delete.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Exact n8n workflow id. When omitted, the workflow is matched semantically.

actions.descriptionCompressed: n8n workflow lifecycle: activate, deactivate, delete.

exampleCalls.user: Use WORKFLOW_LIFECYCLE_OP with the provided parameters.
